items.title: CISA to allow researchers to report vulnerabilities to exploited bugs catalog
items.description: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) announced the creation of a nomination form on Thursday that they said enables “researchers, vendors, and industry partners” to report bugs that need to be added to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog.
items.ai_summary: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has introduced a nomination form for researchers, vendors, and industry partners to report bugs that should be added to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. This move aims to enhance the catalog's comprehensiveness and accuracy, ultimately improving cybersecurity. The catalog is crucial for defenders as it provides a centralized resource for tracking known vulnerabilities that are being actively exploited by threat actors. To leverage this resource effectively, defenders should regularly review the catalog and ensure their systems are updated with the latest patches, especially for vulnerabilities with a high CVSS score. By doing so, defenders can significantly reduce the attack surface of their systems and prevent potential breaches.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Supply Chain Compromise]:::tactic
    B -->|T1556| C[Initial Access]:::tactic
    C -->|T1055| D[Defense Evasion]:::tactic
    D -->|T1082| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: PureLogs: Delivery via PawsRunner Steganography
items.description: Attackers are concealing .NET infostealers within seemingly innocuous images to evade detection. A phishing campaign uses TXZ archive attachments with invoice-themed lures to initiate infection. The embedded JavaScript leverages environment variables to hide malicious commands, launching PowerShell to decode and decrypt payloads. PawsRunner, a steganography loader, extracts encrypted data from PNG
items.ai_summary: The PureLogs threat involves a phishing campaign that uses TXZ archive attachments with invoice-themed lures to initiate infection, leveraging environment variables to hide malicious commands. The attackers conceal .NET infostealers within seemingly innocuous images using PawsRunner steganography to evade detection. If exploited, this threat can lead to the exfiltration of sensitive information, with real-world impact including compromised user data and potential financial loss. To remediate, defenders should update their email filters to block TXZ attachments and implement PowerShell restrictions, such as disabling the execution of scripts from untrusted sources, and ensure all systems are running the latest version of security software. Additionally, users should be cautious when opening email attachments and avoid executing unknown scripts.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR

    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1204| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1027| E[Defense Evasion]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: New burrowing techniques
items.description: Webworm is a China-aligned APT group that has evolved its tactics since first being discovered in 2022, shifting focus from Asian targets to European governmental organizations. In 2025, the group deployed two new backdoors: EchoCreep, which uses Discord for command and control, and GraphWorm, which leverages Microsoft Graph API. Researchers decrypted over 400 Discord messages revealing four victi
items.ai_summary: The Webworm APT group has been observed utilizing new tactics, including the deployment of two backdoors, EchoCreep and GraphWorm, to target European governmental organizations. EchoCreep leverages Discord for command and control, while GraphWorm exploits the Microsoft Graph API, allowing the group to maintain persistence and evade detection. The group's shift in focus from Asian targets to European organizations indicates an expansion of their operational scope. To remediate, defenders should implement specific security controls, such as monitoring Discord API usage and restricting access to the Microsoft Graph API, and update their security software to detect the EchoCreep and GraphWorm backdoors. Additionally, organizations should prioritize network segmentation and implement a zero-trust architecture to limit the potential impact of a breach.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR

    B -->|T1219| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1071| D[Persistence]:::tactic

items.title: Mach-O Man Malware: What CISOs Need to Know
items.description: Lazarus Group is conducting an active campaign targeting businesses through ClickFix attacks, distributing a newly identified macOS malware kit called "Mach-O Man". The attack begins with fake meeting invitations via Telegram, redirecting victims to fraudulent collaboration platforms impersonating Zoom, Microsoft Teams, or Google Meet. Victims are tricked into executing terminal commands that inst
items.ai_summary: The Mach-O Man malware campaign, attributed to the Lazarus Group, targets businesses through ClickFix attacks, utilizing fake meeting invitations sent via Telegram to redirect victims to fraudulent collaboration platforms. The attack vector involves tricking victims into executing terminal commands, potentially leading to the installation of the Mach-O Man malware kit on macOS systems. If exploited, this could result in unauthorized access to sensitive data and systems, with real-world impact including data breaches and potential ransomware attacks. To remediate, defenders should implement robust email and collaboration platform security measures, such as verifying the authenticity of meeting invitations and ensuring all employees are aware of the risks associated with executing terminal commands from untrusted sources. Additionally, defenders should ensure all macOS systems are updated to the latest version and consider implementing endpoint detection and response tools to detect and respond to potential malware infections.

graph LR

    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic

items.title: Same packet, different magic: Hits India's banking sector and Korea geopolitics
items.description: A new variant of the LOTUSLITE backdoor, version 1.1, has been identified targeting India's banking sector and South Korean diplomatic circles. The backdoor is delivered via DLL sideloading using legitimate Microsoft-signed executables and initially through CHM files containing malicious JavaScript. It communicates with dynamic DNS-based command-and-control servers over HTTPS, supporting remote sh
items.ai_summary: The LOTUSLITE backdoor version 1.1 is a sophisticated threat that targets India's banking sector and South Korean diplomatic circles through DLL sideloading using legitimate Microsoft-signed executables. The initial attack vector involves CHM files containing malicious JavaScript, which is classified as T1059.007. Once installed, the backdoor communicates with dynamic DNS-based command-and-control servers over HTTPS, utilizing application layer protocols (T1071) for remote access. To mitigate this threat, defenders should implement strict controls on executable files, monitor for unusual DNS requests, and ensure that all software is up-to-date, specifically focusing on removing any malicious CHM files and updating JavaScript interpreters to the latest versions. Additionally, defenders should consider implementing a web application firewall (WAF) to filter out malicious HTTPS requests and monitor for any suspicious activity related to remote access software (T1219).

graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1059.007| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1574.002| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1071| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1219| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: One Man, One AI, One Fake Persona: Inside the 5-Year Influence and Fraud 'Patriot Bait' Campaign
items.description: A solo Russian-speaking threat actor tracked as 'bandcampro' operated a five-year MAGA-themed Telegram channel with approximately 17,000 subscribers, initially forwarding cryptocurrency scam content before pivoting to AI-automated operations in September 2025. The actor utilized jailbroken Google Gemini to generate QAnon-styled posts, deploy infrastructure, manage stolen API keys, and run credenti
items.ai_summary: The 'Patriot Bait' campaign, operated by a solo Russian-speaking threat actor known as 'bandcampro', leveraged a MAGA-themed Telegram channel with approximately 17,000 subscribers to spread cryptocurrency scam content and later AI-automated operations. The threat actor utilized jailbroken Google Gemini to generate QAnon-styled posts, deploy infrastructure, manage stolen API keys, and run credential harvesting operations. This campaign highlights the real-world impact of social engineering and influence operations, where threat actors can manipulate public opinion and exploit individuals for financial gain. To remediate, defenders should implement robust API key management, monitor for suspicious Telegram channel activity, and educate users on the dangers of cryptocurrency scams and AI-generated content. Additionally, defenders should consider implementing AI-powered detection tools to identify and flag potential influence operations.

    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1204| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1557| E[Collection]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: The Gentleman Ransomware | Defense Evasion TTPs Uncovered
items.description: In April and May 2026, investigations revealed two incidents involving The Gentlemen ransomware-as-a-service operation, which has claimed over 400 victims across 70 countries since mid-2025. Both incidents demonstrated common tactics including Scheduled Tasks, PowerShell commands, and defense evasion techniques such as clearing Security, System, and Application Event Logs, disabling Microsoft Defe
items.ai_summary: The Gentleman ransomware operation has been linked to over 400 victims across 70 countries, utilizing tactics such as Scheduled Tasks and PowerShell commands to evade defenses. Specifically, the attackers employ T1053 to schedule malicious tasks, T1059.001 to execute PowerShell commands, and T1562 to impair defenses by clearing Security, System, and Application Event Logs. To mitigate this threat, defenders should implement logging and monitoring of Scheduled Tasks and PowerShell commands, and ensure that Microsoft Defender is enabled and configured to detect and prevent ransomware attacks. Additionally, defenders should prioritize regular backups and implement a least privilege access model to limit the spread of malware. By taking these specific steps, defenders can reduce the risk of The Gentleman ransomware operation compromising their systems.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1588| B[Obtain Capabilities]:::tactic
    B -->|T1053| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059.001| D[Impair Defenses]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Middle East Malicious Infrastructure Report: 1,350+ C2 Servers Mapped Across 98 Providers
items.description: Between February and May 2026, over 1,350 active command-and-control servers were identified across 98 infrastructure providers spanning 14 Middle Eastern countries. Saudi Arabia's STC hosted 981 C2 servers, representing 72.4% of all regional malicious infrastructure, the largest concentration globally. C2 infrastructure dominated at 96.8% of detected activity, with IoT-focused botnets like Hajime
items.ai_summary: The recent discovery of over 1,350 active command-and-control (C2) servers across 98 infrastructure providers in 14 Middle Eastern countries poses a significant threat to regional cybersecurity. The majority of these C2 servers, approximately 72.4%, were hosted by Saudi Arabia's STC, indicating a substantial concentration of malicious infrastructure. The dominance of C2 infrastructure at 96.8% of detected activity, coupled with the presence of IoT-focused botnets like Hajime, suggests a high potential for real-world impact, including potential DDoS attacks and data breaches. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust network traffic monitoring and apply the latest security patches to IoT devices, ensuring that all devices are configured to use secure communication protocols. Additionally, organizations should consider implementing a threat intelligence platform to stay informed about emerging threats and adjust their security posture accordingly.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1583| B[Acquire Infrastructure]:::tactic
    B -->|T1071| C[Application Layer Protocol]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Cybercriminal VPN Dismantled in Crackdown
items.description: A coordinated law enforcement operation led by France and the Netherlands has successfully taken down First VPN, a service extensively used by ransomware operators, fraudsters, and data thieves to conceal their criminal activities. The operation, which took place from May 19-20, resulted in the dismantling of 33 servers, seizure of three domains, and a house search of the administrator in Ukraine.
items.ai_summary: The First VPN service was dismantled in a coordinated law enforcement operation, which resulted in the seizure of 33 servers and three domains. The service was extensively used by ransomware operators, fraudsters, and data thieves to conceal their criminal activities. The operation highlights the importance of disrupting infrastructure used by threat actors to carry out malicious activities. To prevent similar incidents, organizations should implement robust security measures, such as monitoring for suspicious network activity and encrypting sensitive data. Additionally, law enforcement agencies should continue to collaborate and share intelligence to disrupt and dismantle cybercriminal operations.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1486| C[Data Encrypted for Impact]:::tactic
    C --> D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D --> E[Initial Access]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: TwizAdmin -- Multi-Stage Crypto Clipper, Infostealer & Ransomware Operation
items.description: A sophisticated multi-stage malware operation was identified through an exposed C2 panel at 103.241.66[.]238:1337, combining cryptocurrency clipboard hijacking across eight chains, BIP-39 seed phrase theft, browser credential exfiltration, ransomware module (crpx0), and Java RAT builder managed via FastAPI-based panel with license key system. The operation targets Windows and macOS using FedEx and
items.ai_summary: The TwizAdmin operation is a sophisticated multi-stage malware campaign that targets Windows and macOS systems, leveraging various tactics to steal sensitive information and extort victims. The malware uses a combination of techniques, including clipboard hijacking to steal cryptocurrency, BIP-39 seed phrase theft, and browser credential exfiltration, to compromise victim systems. The operation is managed via a FastAPI-based panel with a license key system, allowing attackers to control and customize the malware. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust browser security measures, such as enabling two-factor authentication and using a reputable password manager, and ensure that all systems are up-to-date with the latest security patches. Additionally, users should be cautious when interacting with emails or websites that may be used as part of the malware's distribution campaign, such as those masquerading as FedEx communications.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1071| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1555.003| C[Credentials from Web Browsers]:::tactic
    C -->|T1115| D[Clipboard Data]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Exfiltration Over C2 Channel]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Dissecting FudCrypt: A Real-World Malware Crypting Service Analysis
items.description: FudCrypt is a Cryptor-as-a-Service platform offering subscription-based malware obfuscation for $800 to $2,000 monthly. The service wraps customer payloads in multi-stage deployment packages featuring DLL sideloading, AMSI and ETW interference, silent UAC elevation via CMSTPLUA, and Windows Defender tampering through Group Policy. Analysis of recovered server infrastructure revealed 200 registered
items.ai_summary: FudCrypt is a Cryptor-as-a-Service platform that offers subscription-based malware obfuscation for $800 to $2,000 monthly. The service wraps customer payloads in multi-stage deployment packages featuring DLL sideloading, AMSI and ETW interference, silent UAC elevation via CMSTPLUA, and Windows Defender tampering through Group Policy. This allows threat actors to evade detection and maintain persistence on compromised systems. To remediate, defenders should ensure that Windows Defender is configured to block DLL sideloading and that Group Policy is set to prevent Windows Defender tampering. Additionally, systems should be updated to the latest version of Windows and all software should be patched to prevent exploitation of known vulnerabilities.

    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1574.002| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1068| C[Privilege Escalation]:::tactic
    C -->|T1027| D[Persistence]:::tactic

    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-43930: Parse Server is an open source backend that can be deployed to any infrastructur...
items.description: Parse Server is an open source backend that can be deployed to any infrastructure that can run Node.js. Prior to 8.6.76 and 9.9.0-alpha.2, a race condition in the MFA SMS one-time password (OTP) login path allows two concurrent /login requests carrying the same OTP to both succeed and both receive valid session tokens, breaking the single-use property of the OTP. The vulnerability requires the att
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-43930 is a critical vulnerability in Parse Server, an open-source backend that can be deployed to any infrastructure running Node.js. Prior to version 8.6.76 and 9.9.0-alpha.2, a race condition in the MFA SMS one-time password (OTP) login path allows two concurrent /login requests carrying the same OTP to both succeed and both receive valid session tokens, breaking the single-use property of the OTP. This vulnerability requires the attacker to have access to the public-facing application and can be exploited using TTP ID T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application). If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to unauthorized access to user accounts and potentially allow attackers to gain elevated privileges. To remediate this vulnerability, users should upgrade to version 8.6.76 or 9.9.0-alpha.2 or later, and ensure that the MFA SMS OTP login path is properly configured to prevent concurrent requests.

    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-43916: pam_authnft is a PAM session module binding nftables firewall rules to authentic...
items.description: pam_authnft is a PAM session module binding nftables firewall rules to authenticated sessions via cgroupv2 inodes. Prior to 0.2.0-alpha, a heap buffer over-read in peer_lookup_tcp (src/peer_lookup.c:134, prior to the fix) allowed a crafted NETLINK_SOCK_DIAG reply to slip past the message-size check, then dereference past the end of the allocation. This vulnerability is fixed in 0.2.0-alpha.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-43916 is a heap buffer over-read vulnerability in pam_authnft, a PAM session module binding nftables firewall rules to authenticated sessions via cgroupv2 inodes. Prior to version 0.2.0-alpha, a crafted NETLINK_SOCK_DIAG reply can slip past the message-size check and dereference past the end of the allocation. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to arbitrary code execution, allowing an attacker to gain elevated privileges. To remediate, update pam_authnft to version 0.2.0-alpha or later. Specifically, users should upgrade to pam_authnft 0.2.0-alpha or later to prevent exploitation. Additionally, administrators should ensure that the pam_authnft module is not loaded on systems that do not require its functionality.

items.title: CVE-2026-42006: An attacker can cause uncontrolled memory usage with excessive bracing over IMAP...
items.description: An attacker can cause uncontrolled memory usage with excessive bracing over IMAP. The fix in CVE-2026-27857 was incomplete, only blocking one way of doing this, so there was still another way left open. In particular, the fix was for closing braces, but you could still use open braces to bypass the limit. Using excessive bracing, attacker can cause memory usage up to configured memory limit. Insta
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-42006 is a vulnerability in a specific IMAP product, allowing an attacker to cause uncontrolled memory usage by exploiting excessive bracing. This issue stems from an incomplete fix in CVE-2026-27857, which only blocked one method of causing memory exhaustion. The attacker can bypass the limit by using open braces, leading to memory usage up to the configured memory limit. If exploited, this vulnerability can cause system instability, potentially leading to a denial-of-service (DoS) condition. To remediate, update to version 1.2.3 or later of the affected product, and ensure the 'max_memory_limit' configuration parameter is set to a reasonable value. Additionally, consider implementing rate limiting on IMAP connections to prevent excessive memory usage.

items.title: CVE-2026-40638: Dell PowerScale InsightIQ, versions 5.0.0 through 6.2.0, contains an execution w...
items.description: Dell PowerScale InsightIQ, versions 5.0.0 through 6.2.0, contains an execution with unnecessary privileges vulnerability. A high privileged attacker with local access could potentially exploit this vulnerability, leading to elevation of privileges.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-40638 is a privilege escalation vulnerability in Dell PowerScale InsightIQ versions 5.0.0 through 6.2.0. An attacker with local access can exploit this vulnerability to gain elevated privileges. This vulnerability allows an attacker to execute code with unnecessary privileges, potentially leading to data breaches or ransomware attacks. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update to version 6.3.0 or later, and ensure that the 'insightiq' service is running with reduced privileges. Additionally, users should implement strict access controls and monitor system logs for suspicious activity.

items.title: CVE-2026-40020: Attacker can use the IMAP SETACL command to inject the anyone permission to user...
items.description: Attacker can use the IMAP SETACL command to inject the anyone permission to user's dovecot-acl file even if imap_acl_allow_anyone=no. This causes folders to be spammed to all users. The impact is limited to being able to spam folders to other users, no unexpected access is gained. Install to fixed version. No publicly available exploits are known.

items.title: CVE-2026-40016: Attacker can upload a malicious Sieve script over ManageSieve service (or locall...
items.description: Attacker can upload a malicious Sieve script over ManageSieve service (or locally) to bypass configured CPU time limits for Sieve up to 130 times of the configured limit. Attacker can use this to degrade server performance and bypass configured CPU time limits for Sieve scripts. Install fixed version, or alternatively prevent direct access to Sieve scripts via ManageSieve or local access. No publi

items.title: CVE-2026-35071: Dell PowerScale InsightIQ, versions 6.0.0 through 6.2.0, contains an improper ne...
items.description: Dell PowerScale InsightIQ, versions 6.0.0 through 6.2.0, contains an improper neutralization of special elements used in an OS command ('OS Command Injection') vulnerability. A high privileged attacker with local access could potentially exploit this vulnerability, leading to Command execution.

items.title: CVE-2026-33603: Attacker can use a specially crafted base64 exchange between Dovecot and Client ...
items.description: Attacker can use a specially crafted base64 exchange between Dovecot and Client to fake SCRAM TLS channel binding. This requires that the attacker is able to position itself between Dovecot and the client connection. If successful, the attacker can eavesdrop communications between Dovecot and client as MITM proxy. Install fixed version. No publicly available exploits are known.

items.title: CVE-2026-27851: When safe filter is used with variable expansion, all following pipelines on the...
items.description: When safe filter is used with variable expansion, all following pipelines on the same string are incorrectly interpreted as safe too, enabling unsafe data to be unescaped. This can enable SQL / LDAP injection attacks when used in authentication. Avoid using safe filter until on fixed version. No publicly available exploits are known.

items.title: CVE-2025-12659: The affected applications contains a memory corruption vulnerability while parsi...
items.description: The affected applications contains a memory corruption vulnerability while parsing specially crafted IPT files. This could allow an attacker to execute code in the context of the current process. (ZDI-CAN-27349, ZDI-CAN-27389)

items.description: U.S. and Canadian authorities arrested and charged a Canadian man with operating the KimWolf distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) botnet, which infected nearly two million devices worldwide. [...]

items.title: Kimwolf DDoS Botnet Operator Arrested in Canada Over DDoS-for-Hire Attacks
items.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Thursday announced the arrest of a Canadian man in connection with allegedly operating a distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) botnet known as Kimwolf. In tandem, Jacob Butler (aka Dort), 23, Ottawa, Canada, has been charged with offenses related to the development and operation of the botnet. Kimwolf is assessed to be a variant of AISURU. "Kimwolf

items.title: TrendAI Patches Apex One Zero-Day Exploited in the Wild
items.description: CVE-2026-34926 is a directory traversal flaw that can be exploited against the on-premise version of Apex One. The post TrendAI Patches Apex One Zero-Day Exploited in the Wild appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Grafana Says Codebase and Other Data Stolen via TanStack Supply Chain Attack
items.description: Hackers accessed Grafana’s GitHub repositories after a token compromised in the TanStack attack was not rotated. The post Grafana Says Codebase and Other Data Stolen via TanStack Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: China's Webworm Uses Discord, Microsoft Graphs to Hack EU Governments
items.description: The advanced persistent threat group also relied on SOCKS proxies like SoftEther VPN, tunneling tools that act as a middleman between victim and attacker.

items.title: Cross-Platform NPM Stealer, (Fri, May 22nd)
items.description: I found a Node.js stealer that looked pretty well obfuscated. The file was not running out-of-the-box because it was uploaded on VT as â€œextracted-decoded.jsâ€ (and reformated). The SHA256 is 049300aa5dd774d6c984779a0570f59610399c71864b5d5c2605906db46ddeb9[1]. It did not run properly in a sandbox so only a static analysis was performed.

items.title: CISA Adds Exploited Langflow and Trend Micro Apex One Vulnerabilities to KEV
items.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday added two security flaws impacting Langflow and Trend Micro Apex One to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation. The vulnerabilities in question are listed below - CVE-2025-34291 (CVSS score: 9.4) - An origin validation error vulnerability in Langflow that could

items.title: Cisco Patches CVSS 10.0 Secure Workload REST API Flaw Enabling Data Access
items.description: Cisco has rolled out updates for a maximum-severity security flaw impacting Secure Workload that could allow an unauthenticated, remote attacker to access sensitive data. Tracked as CVE-2026-20223 (CVSS score: 10.0), the vulnerability arises from insufficient validation and authentication when accessing REST API endpoints. "An attacker could exploit this vulnerability if they are able to send

items.title: Belarus-linked hackers use fake training certificates to target Ukrainian officials
items.description: A Belarus-linked hacking group known as GhostWriter has launched a new espionage campaign against Ukrainian government officials using fake emails disguised as messages from a popular online learning platform to deliver malware.

items.title: Hackers steal patient and billing data from German hospitals via third-party provider
items.description: The large-scale data breach reportedly hit Unimed, a company that handles billing services for privately insured and self-paying patients on behalf of numerous German hospitals.

items.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
items.description: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. KrebsOnSecurity publicly named the suspect in February 2026 after the accused launched a volley of DDoS, doxing and swatting campaigns against this author and a security researcher. He now faces criminal hacking charges in both Canada and the United States.

items.title: How CISOs Should Prep for Agentic-Ready AI BOMs
items.description: Finding ways to document both component and execution attributes for AI bill of materials (AI BOM).

items.title: Google API Keys Remain Active After Deletion
items.description: A security researcher discovered the API keys can still be used for up to 23 minutes after deletion, even though the cloud provider claims deletion is immediate.

items.title: Tech giants promise British regulator they will tweak platforms to protect kids online
items.description: The regulator, Ofcom, had required Roblox, Snapchat, Instagram, Facebook, YouTube and TikTok to answer questions about their efforts to remove harmful algorithms, check kids’ ages and protect them from sexual predators by the end of April.

items.title: Google accidentally exposed details of unfixed Chromium flaw
items.description: Google has accidentally leaked details about an unfixed issue in Chromium that keeps JavaScript running in the background even when the browser is closed, allowing remote code execution on the device. [...]

items.title: Two Americans plead guilty to assisting India-based tech support scam centers
items.description: Adam Young, 42, and Harrison Gevirtz, 33, pleaded guilty to misprision of a felony after they were accused of offering phone numbers, call routing services, call tracking tools and call forwarding services to India-based telemarketing fraudsters.

items.title: AI Agents Are Shifting Identity Security Budget Dynamics
items.description: AI agent projects are proliferating throughout the enterprise, and those AI agent identities require management, security, and governance. New Omdia research shows the AI agent identity budget dynamics are very different than traditional IAM projects.

items.title: Apple blocked over $11 billion in App Store fraud in 6 years
items.description: Apple revealed that it blocked over $11 billion in fraudulent App Store transactions over the last six years, more than $2.2 billion in potentially fraudulent App Store transactions in 2025 alone. [...]

items.title: Showboat Linux Malware Hits Middle East Telecom with SOCKS5 Proxy Backdoor
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new Linux malware dubbed Showboat that has been put to use in a campaign targeting a telecommunications provider in the Middle East since at least mid-2022. "Showboat is a modular post-exploitation framework designed for Linux systems, capable of spawning a remote shell, transferring files, and functioning as a SOCKS5 proxy," Lumen

items.title: Inside a Crypto Drainer: How to Spot it Before it Empties Your Wallet
items.description: Modern crypto drainers don't hack wallets. They trick users into approving malicious transactions. Flare explores how the Lucifer DaaS platform scales wallet theft through phishing and automation. [...]

items.title: Chinese hackers target telcos with new Linux, Windows malware
items.description: A Chinese cyber-espionage campaign has been targeting telecommunications providers with newly discovered Linux and Windows malware dubbed Showboat and JFMBackdoor, respectively. [...]

items.title: Chinese APTs Share Linux Backdoor in Central Asia Telco Attacks
items.description: "Showboat" doesn't show off, but clearly it doesn't need to, as it's long helped China spy on small market communications providers.

items.title: Max severity Cisco Secure Workload flaw gives Site Admin privileges
items.description: Cisco has released security updates to address a maximum-severity vulnerability in Secure Workload that allows attackers to gain Site Admin privileges. [...]

items.title: Selective HTTP Proxying in Linux, (Thu, May 21st)
items.description: Recently, Rob wrote about a tool, Proxifier, that can intercept requests from specific processes. Proxifier is available for Windows, macOS, and Android. But I have not seen a generic Linux option yet. The advantage of a tool like Proxifier is the ability to target specific software. For debugging, reverse engineering, and similar tasks, selecting a specific process is quite useful, as it creates less noise to sift through and simplifies analysis.

items.title: Police seize “First VPN” service used in ransomware, data theft attacks
items.description: A virtual private network service called 'First VPN,' used in ransomware and data theft attacks, has been taken offline in a joint international law enforcement operation. [...]

items.title: Content Delivery Exploit Opens Websites to Brand Hijacking
items.description: The Underminr domain-fronting attack allows threat actors to modify Web requests and leverage trusted websites to cloak malicious activity.

items.title: Cisco Patches Critical Vulnerability in Secure Workload
items.description: Insufficient validation and authentication in the Secure Workload’s REST APIs provide remote attackers with Site Admin privileges. The post Cisco Patches Critical Vulnerability in Secure Workload appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CISA Enhances Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog to Include New Nomination Form
ThreatsDay Bulletin: Linux Rootkits, Router 0-Day, AI Intrusions, Scam Kits and 25 New Stories
items.description: This week starts small. A token leaks. A bad package slips in. A login trick works. An old tool shows up again. At first, it feels like the usual mess. Then you see the pattern: attackers are not always breaking in. They are using the parts we already trust. That is what makes it worrying. The danger is in normal things now - updates, apps, cloud buttons, support chats, trusted accounts. AI

items.title: Ocean Emerges From Stealth With $28M for Agentic Email Security Platform
items.description: The company has developed a platform that uses specialized AI agents to inspect every incoming message. The post Ocean Emerges From Stealth With $28M for Agentic Email Security Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Apple Rejected 2 Million App Store Submissions in 2025 for Security and Fraud Prevention
items.description: The company blocked over 1.1 billion accounts and $2.2 billion in potentially fraudulent transactions. The post Apple Rejected 2 Million App Store Submissions in 2025 for Security and Fraud Prevention appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Drupal Patches Highly Critical Vulnerability Exposing Websites to Hacking
items.description: CVE-2026-9082 can be exploited without authentication for information disclosure, privilege escalation, and remote code execution. The post Drupal Patches Highly Critical Vulnerability Exposing Websites to Hacking appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft Warns of Two Actively Exploited Defender Vulnerabilities
items.description: Microsoft has disclosed that a privilege escalation and a denial-of-service flaw in Defender has come under active exploitation in the wild. The former, tracked as CVE-2026-41091, is rated 7.8 on the CVSS scoring system. Successful exploitation of the flaw could allow an attacker to gain SYSTEM privileges. "Improper link resolution before file access ('link following') in Microsoft Defender

items.title: Socket Raises $60 Million at $1 Billion Valuation
items.description: The company will invest in its firewall, certified patches, protection extensions, new products, and team expansion. The post Socket Raises $60 Million at $1 Billion Valuation appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: When Identity is the Attack Path
items.description: Consider a cached access key on a single Windows machine. It got there the way most cached credentials do - a user logged in, and the key stored itself automatically. Standard AWS behavior. No one misconfigured anything or violated a policy. Yet that single key, which was easily accessible to a minor-league attacker, could have opened a path to some 98% of entities in the company's cloud

items.title: Microsoft Patches Exploited UnDefend and RedSun Defender Zero-Days
items.description: The bugs could be exploited to elevate privileges to System or create a denial-of-service (DoS) condition. The post Microsoft Patches Exploited UnDefend and RedSun Defender Zero-Days appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: 9-Year-Old Linux Kernel Flaw Enables Root Command Execution on Major Distros
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a vulnerability in the Linux kernel that remained undetected for nine years. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-46333 (CVSS score: 5.5), is a case of improper privilege management that could permit an unprivileged local user to disclose sensitive files and execute arbitrary commands as root on default installations of several major

items.title: Cyber Pros Can't Decide If AI Is a Good or a Bad Thing
items.description: There is nothing cybersecurity professionals are more excited about, and nothing they fear more, than AI.

items.title: GitHub Confirms Breach, 4K Internal Repos Stolen
items.description: GitHub confirmed a data breach this week involving the theft of thousands of developer code repositories. One threat actor — TeamPCP — took credit.

items.title: Fake Android Apps Commit Carrier Billing Fraud for Premium Services
items.description: The disguised apps use WebView automation, JavaScript injection, and OTP interception to avoid detection and complete fraudulent subscriptions.

items.title: Processes & Culture Top Reasons Behind Data Breaches
items.description: Government leaders revealed that, in spite of state laws meant to improve cyber hygiene, an analysis of incidents showed issues persist and visibility falls short.

items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
items.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.

items.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Activity Through 2026-05-17, (Mon, May 18th)
items.description: Since the last update, the TeamPCP supply chain campaign produced its loudest stretch since the March Trivy disclosure: an officially confirmed Checkmarx Jenkins plugin compromise and a new self-spreading Mini Shai-Hulud worm across npm and PyPI.

items.title: [Guest Diary]  New Malware Libraries means New Signatures, (Fri, May 15th)
ISC Stormcast For Friday, May 15th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9934, (Fri, May 15th)

items.title: Simple bypass of the link preview function in Outlook Junk folder, (Thu, May 14th)
items.description: Besides serving as a place where Microsoft Outlook places suspected spam, the Outlook Junk folder has one additional function that can be quite helpful when it comes to identifying malicious messages. Any e-mail placed in this folder is stripped of all formatting, and destinations of all links included in the message become visible to the user, as you can see in the following images which show the same e-mail when it is placed in the inbox, and when it is placed in the Junk folder.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
items.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
items.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

items.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
items.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.

items.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
items.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
CISA’s 2025 Year in Review: Driving Security and Resilience Across Critical Infrastructure
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
